task.name: RBTV Help
task.description: Display RBTV skills and deep-dive into any skill to understand its workflows
task.objective: Help users discover and understand RBTV skills by dynamically scanning available skills and explaining their underlying agents, workflows, or tasks.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
No hardcoded information — all skill descriptions come from reading files.
Dynamic discovery — if new skills are added, they appear automatically.
Read before explaining — always load the actual agent/workflow/task file before explaining.
Handle agent files — if the skill loads an agent, read the agent file to find its menu items and workflows.

substep.title: Discover Available Skills
substep.action: For each skill directory found, read its SKILL.md and extract from the frontmatter: name (the skill name) and description (what it does).
Build a numbered list of available skills.

substep.title: Present Skills
substep.action: Present the skills as a numbered menu with format: [N] /{name} — {description}
Include options: Enter a number to deep-dive, Enter 'X' to exit help.
HALT and WAIT for user selection.

substep.title: Read Skill File
substep.action: Load the full SKILL.md for the selected skill.

substep.title: Identify Target
substep.action: From the Activation section, identify what the skill loads: an agent file (via agent-activation block) or a workflow/task file (via "Load and follow" path).

substep.title: Read and Explain Target
substep.action: Load the referenced agent, workflow, or task file.
For agents: extract and explain persona, menu items, and available capabilities.
For workflows/tasks: extract and explain purpose, steps, outputs, and how to use.

substep.title: Present and Return
substep.action: Present the explanation. Offer: Enter another number, 'B' to go back to list, 'X' to exit.
HALT and WAIT for user selection.

halt-conditions.condition: Skills folder is empty — display: "No RBTV skills found. Run bootstrap.py to sync skills."
Target file missing — display: "This skill's target is not yet available. It may be in development."
Unable to parse — show what was found and ask user if they want to see the raw file.

task.exit: When user enters 'X' or 'exit': Thank them and end the help session. Suggest /bmad-help for full BMAD ecosystem help.
